grades.expectation: Response mentions CQS training requirements for all conveyancing staff
grades.reason: Clearly states mandatory CQS training modules requirement under 6.1, specifies completion within 6 months, and mentions core training covering Protocol and AML

grades.expectation: Response covers protocol awareness and compliance training
grades.reason: Dedicated section on Protocol Awareness (Requirements 1.4 & 5.8) covering Law Society Conveyancing Protocol training and firm-specific adoption procedures

grades.expectation: Response mentions supervision requirements for new staff
grades.reason: Section 3 specifically addresses Requirement 3.3 for formal supervisor assignment and documented supervision arrangements with frequency/method specification

grades.expectation: Response references the need to maintain training records as CQS evidence
grades.reason: Explicitly mentions keeping completion certificates in training records, maintaining personnel files with training records, and using induction checklists as evidence

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes CQS firm-level requirements from individual SRA competence
grades.reason: Clear distinction made in opening paragraph and throughout, contrasting CQS practice management standards with SRA individual competence requirements and outcomes-focused approach
